terms.definition: Tabular count of classifier predictions vs true labels. For binary tasks, a 2×2 table with cells TP, FP, TN, FN at one threshold. Canonical definition and layout in this topic.
Count of examples where the true label is positive (class 1) and the model predicted positive. A correct detection of the positive class.
Count of examples where the true label is negative (class 0) but the model predicted positive. A false alarm — Type I error in binary testing.
Count of examples where the true label is negative and the model predicted negative. A correct rejection of the positive class.
Count of examples where the true label is positive but the model predicted negative. A miss — Type II error in binary testing.
The label treated as 1 (the event of interest) when defining TP/FP/TN/FN — must be stated explicitly; swapping positive/negative swaps FP and FN interpretations.
General name for cross-tabulating two categorical variables; the confusion matrix is the contingency table of actual vs predicted class labels.
